goal: Make the corridor readable and high-fidelity from a player perspective using lighting, post-processing, shaders, texturing, and screenshot validation.

rootIdeas.hypothesis: Triplanar mapping will make walls, floors, ceilings, and props easier to texture without UV seams or stretched procedural maps.
rootIdeas.whyItMatters: Current generated textures are present but still read as broad dark surfaces. Projection by world normal should let brick, damp concrete, grime, and metal panels wrap consistently across modular surfaces.

subtests.test: Apply a green-black worn brick/block shader to wall surfaces only.
acceptance: Wall silhouette remains horror-dark but visible.
Brick/block pattern is readable in spawn and movement screenshots.
No obvious texture stretching on corners.

subtests.test: Apply damp concrete with roughness and grime variation to floor/terrain surfaces only.
acceptance: Wet/grime breakup is visible near camera.

subtests.test: Apply dark ceiling service-panel breakup without making the upper frame noisy.
acceptance: Upper frame is no longer blank black.
Ceiling does not compete with route direction.

risks: Too much texture contrast can flatten fog and destroy horror mood.
Shader complexity can hurt frame time if applied naively to every instanced mesh.
World-space projection must keep deterministic seeds so screenshots remain comparable.

rootIdeas.hypothesis: A real renderer-owned exposure/gamma/vignette/noise pass will control readability better than CSS overlay effects.

subtests.test: Lift midtones while preserving black horror shadows.
acceptance: Human view does not look washed out.
Props and walls remain legible during movement.

subtests.test: Apply subtle noise after exposure so darkness has texture instead of flat black.
acceptance: Noise is visible only as atmosphere.
It does not obscure UI-free gameplay or route visibility.

risks: Post-processing can pass metrics while still feeling muddy.
CSS overlay and renderer post-process can double-darken if both remain active.

work: Split static world materials by surface domain: wall, floor, ceiling, trim.
Add first triplanar shader for wall brick/block pattern.
Add screenshot-gated comparison for visible wall texture in spawn and movement frames.
